question.text: Under Section 212 of the Companies Act, 2013, which of the following can trigger an SFIO investigation?

answer.text: Receipt of a report from the Registrar or Inspector
A special resolution passed by the company requesting investigation
Request from any Department of Central or State Government

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Section 212(1) of the Companies Act, 2013, the Central Government may assign investigation to SFIO on: (a) receipt of ROC/Inspector report under section 208, (b) special resolution by company, (c) in public interest, or (d) request from any Central/State Government department. **The Trap:** All four triggers are valid — there is no single exclusive trigger. Each independently can initiate an SFIO investigation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** SFIO is a multi-disciplinary body under MCA for serious corporate fraud. It has forensic auditing, law, IT, and capital market experts. Know all four investigation triggers.
